title: Calamagrostis humilis
intro: Calamagrostis humilis, also known as Dwarf Brome Grass, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its slender stems and feathery foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Calamagrostis humilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Calamagrostis humilis is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It is a type of tussock grass native to China. Calamagrostis humilis is further grouped into the subfamily Pooideae and tribe Poeae. This classification places it among the cool-season grasses. The specific epithet 'humilis' translates to 'dwarf' or 'low-growing', describing its growth habit.
uses: Calamagrostis humilis is a plant species with potential medicinal uses, although limited research is available. More research is needed to confirm the plant's potential as a medicinal resource.
